node tools/validate-contracts.mjs passes
impeccable critique >=7/10, zero P0 findings (qa gate)
cohort-runner.mjs produces same KPIs across two runs with seed=42 (determinism)

All critical states present: empty catalog, not-found, payment failure, empty orders

Contract gate: node tools/validate-contracts.mjs (hard gate)

evidence: App root: apps/video-storefront/ (Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind)

path_specificity.notes: Every file path is specified. No ambiguity about where things live.

task_granularity.notes: Tasks are granular and ordered. Each is a discrete commit-worthy unit.

evidence: T2 (tokens) must complete before T3 (components) — components reference or-* CSS variables
T4 (lib/films.ts with catalog) must complete before T5 (routes) — routes import getFilm()
T1 (scaffold) must complete before all others — package.json defines the Next.js version
ATM-416 foundation must be complete (it is: commit 164785a) — cohort-runner needs commerce-core
dependency_awareness.notes: Dependency chain is clear. All ATM-416 dependencies are satisfied.

evidence: Engineering proof: npm run build output + validate-contracts output → proof/engineering-eval-result.json
QA proof: impeccable critique result → proof/qa-eval-result.json
KPI proof: two cohort runs → proof/baseline/ (two runs, events array comparison)

proof_quality.notes: Each stage produces a durable on-disk proof artifact. No self-attestation without evidence.

overall_notes: Plan is specific, dependency-ordered, and verifiable. Two hard gates (build + contracts) run automatically. The verbatim-port constraint is the key simplification: no design decisions to make, just wiring. validate-contracts.mjs is already green (ATM-416).
